content.text:
Based on the zoomed view of atom 15 (N:15), I can clearly identify its covalently connected neighbour atoms. The nitrogen atom (N:15) is part of a heterocyclic ring structure.

**Neighbour atoms of atom 15 (N):**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 10 | C (Carbon) |
| 16 | C (Carbon) |

**geomconf_index:** 85

Atom 15 (Nitrogen) is covalently bonded to two carbon atoms: C:10 (which is part of the fused benzene ring system) and C:16 (which connects to another nitrogen N:17 in the 5-membered heterocyclic ring). The nitrogen forms part of a thiophene-like ring fused with a benzene ring system.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 15, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
